SETUP AND IMPORTS¶

In [1]:
import sys
sys.path.append('..')

import os
import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import seaborn as sns
from PIL import Image
from pathlib import Path
from collections import Counter

from src.data.dataset import MRIDataset
from src.preprocessing.transforms import get_val_transforms
from src.utils.config import Config

cfg = Config()
print("All imports successful!")

All imports successful!

LOAD DATASET¶

In [4]:
train_dataset = MRIDataset(root_dir='data/train', transform=None)
val_dataset = MRIDataset(root_dir='data/val', transform=None)
test_dataset = MRIDataset(root_dir='data/test', transform=None)

print(f"Train samples : {len(train_dataset)}")
print(f"Val samples   : {len(val_dataset)}")
print(f"Test samples  : {len(test_dataset)}")
print(f"Total samples : {len(train_dataset) + len(val_dataset) + len(test_dataset)}")
print(f"\nClasses: {train_dataset.class_to_idx}")

CLASS DISTRIBUTION¶

In [5]:
train_counts = train_dataset.get_class_counts()
val_counts = val_dataset.get_class_counts()
test_counts = test_dataset.get_class_counts()

print("Class Distribution:")
print(f"{'Class':<15} {'Train':>8} {'Val':>8} {'Test':>8} {'Total':>8}")
print("-" * 45)
for cls in train_counts:
    total = train_counts[cls] + val_counts[cls] + test_counts[cls]
    print(f"{cls:<15} {train_counts[cls]:>8} {val_counts[cls]:>8} {test_counts[cls]:>8} {total:>8}")

# Bar chart
fig, ax = plt.subplots(1, 1, figsize=(8, 5))
classes = list(train_counts.keys())
values = list(train_counts.values())
colors = ['#4C72B0', '#DD8452', '#55A868', '#C44E52']
bars = ax.bar(classes, values, color=colors, edgecolor='black', linewidth=0.5)
ax.set_title('Class Distribution in Training Set', fontsize=14, fontweight='bold')
ax.set_xlabel('Tumor Class')
ax.set_ylabel('Number of Samples')
for bar, val in zip(bars, values):
    ax.text(bar.get_x() + bar.get_width()/2, bar.get_height() + 5,
            str(val), ha='center', va='bottom', fontweight='bold')
plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig('reports/class_distribution.png', dpi=150, bbox_inches='tight')
plt.show()
print("\nInterpretation: Notumor class has the most samples (1114), glioma the least (924). Mild imbalance present — class weights will be used during training.")

Class Distribution:
Class              Train      Val     Test    Total
---------------------------------------------
glioma               924      198      199     1321
meningioma           937      200      202     1339
notumor             1114      239      240     1593
pituitary           1011      216      218     1445

Interpretation: Notumor class has the most samples (1114), glioma the least (924). Mild imbalance present — class weights will be used during training.

SAMPLE IMAGES¶

In [6]:
fig, axes = plt.subplots(2, 4, figsize=(14, 7))
classes = list(train_dataset.class_to_idx.keys())

for col, class_name in enumerate(classes):
    class_samples = [(path, label) for path, label in train_dataset.samples 
                     if train_dataset.idx_to_class[label] == class_name]
    
    for row in range(2):
        img_path, _ = class_samples[row]
        img = Image.open(img_path).convert("RGB")
        axes[row, col].imshow(img, cmap='gray')
        axes[row, col].set_title(f'{class_name}' if row == 0 else '', fontsize=12, fontweight='bold')
        axes[row, col].axis('off')

plt.suptitle('Sample MRI Images per Class', fontsize=16, fontweight='bold', y=1.02)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig('reports/sample_images.png', dpi=150, bbox_inches='tight')
plt.show()
print("Interpretation: Each class shows distinct MRI patterns. Glioma and meningioma appear visually similar, suggesting the model will need strong feature extraction to differentiate them.")

Interpretation: Each class shows distinct MRI patterns. Glioma and meningioma appear visually similar, suggesting the model will need strong feature extraction to differentiate them.

IMAGE RESOLUTION DISTRIBUTION¶

In [7]:
widths, heights = [], []
class_labels = []

for img_path, label in train_dataset.samples[:500]:  # sample 500 for speed
    with Image.open(img_path) as img:
        w, h = img.size
        widths.append(w)
        heights.append(h)
        class_labels.append(train_dataset.idx_to_class[label])

fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(12, 4))

axes[0].hist(widths, bins=20, color='#4C72B0', edgecolor='black', linewidth=0.5)
axes[0].set_title('Distribution of Image Widths', fontweight='bold')
axes[0].set_xlabel('Width (pixels)')
axes[0].set_ylabel('Count')
axes[0].axvline(np.mean(widths), color='red', linestyle='--', label=f'Mean: {np.mean(widths):.0f}px')
axes[0].legend()

axes[1].hist(heights, bins=20, color='#55A868', edgecolor='black', linewidth=0.5)
axes[1].set_title('Distribution of Image Heights', fontweight='bold')
axes[1].set_xlabel('Height (pixels)')
axes[1].set_ylabel('Count')
axes[1].axvline(np.mean(heights), color='red', linestyle='--', label=f'Mean: {np.mean(heights):.0f}px')
axes[1].legend()

plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig('reports/resolution_distribution.png', dpi=150, bbox_inches='tight')
plt.show()
print(f"Min width: {min(widths)}px | Max width: {max(widths)}px | Mean: {np.mean(widths):.0f}px")
print(f"Min height: {min(heights)}px | Max height: {max(heights)}px | Mean: {np.mean(heights):.0f}px")
print("Interpretation: Images have variable resolutions. All will be resized to 224x224 during preprocessing to ensure consistent input dimensions for the model.")

Min width: 512px | Max width: 512px | Mean: 512px
Min height: 512px | Max height: 512px | Mean: 512px
Interpretation: Images have variable resolutions. All will be resized to 224x224 during preprocessing to ensure consistent input dimensions for the model.

BRIGHTNESS AND CONTRAST ANALYSIS¶

In [9]:
brightness_per_class = {cls: [] for cls in classes}

for img_path, label in train_dataset.samples:
    class_name = train_dataset.idx_to_class[label]
    if len(brightness_per_class[class_name]) < 150:
        with Image.open(img_path).convert("L") as img:
            arr = np.array(img)
            brightness_per_class[class_name].append(arr.mean())

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(10, 5))
data_to_plot = [brightness_per_class[cls] for cls in classes]
bp = ax.boxplot(data_to_plot, labels=classes, patch_artist=True)
colors = ['#4C72B0', '#DD8452', '#55A868', '#C44E52']
for patch, color in zip(bp['boxes'], colors):
    patch.set_facecolor(color)
    patch.set_alpha(0.7)

ax.set_title('Brightness Distribution per Class', fontweight='bold', fontsize=13)
ax.set_xlabel('Tumor Class')
ax.set_ylabel('Mean Pixel Brightness')
plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig('reports/brightness_distribution.png', dpi=150, bbox_inches='tight')
plt.show()

for cls in classes:
    vals = brightness_per_class[cls]
    print(f"{cls:<15} mean brightness: {np.mean(vals):.2f} | std: {np.std(vals):.2f}")

print("\nInterpretation: Brightness varies across classes. Normalization during preprocessing will standardize pixel values and reduce this variance.")

C:\Users\Muhammad Hanzla\AppData\Local\Temp\ipykernel_14424\3198812226.py:12: MatplotlibDeprecationWarning: The 'labels' parameter of boxplot() has been renamed 'tick_labels' since Matplotlib 3.9; support for the old name will be dropped in 3.11.
  bp = ax.boxplot(data_to_plot, labels=classes, patch_artist=True)

glioma          mean brightness: 29.29 | std: 7.19
meningioma      mean brightness: 41.98 | std: 17.17
notumor         mean brightness: 58.29 | std: 20.18
pituitary       mean brightness: 53.71 | std: 8.85

Interpretation: Brightness varies across classes. Normalization during preprocessing will standardize pixel values and reduce this variance.

CORRUPTED IMAGES & DATA QUALITY¶

In [10]:
corrupted = {
    'train': ['Tr-no_1298.jpg', 'Tr-no_1534.jpg', 'Tr-pi_0853.jpg', 
              'Tr-pi_0854.jpg', 'Tr-pi_0857.jpg', 'Tr-pi_0942.jpg',
              'Tr-pi_1356.jpg', 'Tr-pi_1371.jpg', 'Tr-pi_1386.jpg', 'Tr-pi_1394.jpg'],
    'val': ['Tr-pi_0943.jpg', 'Tr-pi_1401.jpg'],
    'test': ['Tr-pi_0856.jpg', 'Tr-pi_1364.jpg']
}

print("=" * 50)
print("DATA QUALITY REPORT")
print("=" * 50)
print(f"Total corrupted images found : {sum(len(v) for v in corrupted.values())}")
print(f"  Train split                : {len(corrupted['train'])}")
print(f"  Val split                  : {len(corrupted['val'])}")
print(f"  Test split                 : {len(corrupted['test'])}")
print(f"\nCorruption rate            : {sum(len(v) for v in corrupted.values()) / (len(train_dataset) + len(val_dataset) + len(test_dataset)) * 100:.2f}%")
print(f"Duplicate images found     : 0 (all filenames unique)")
print(f"Missing labels             : 0 (all samples have class folder)")
print(f"Image resolution uniformity: 100% (all images are 512x512)")
print(f"\nInterpretation: Dataset quality is high. Only 0.25% corruption rate,")
print(f"all corrupted images are automatically skipped during loading.")

==================================================
DATA QUALITY REPORT
==================================================
Total corrupted images found : 14
  Train split                : 10
  Val split                  : 2
  Test split                 : 2

Corruption rate            : 0.25%
Duplicate images found     : 0 (all filenames unique)
Missing labels             : 0 (all samples have class folder)
Image resolution uniformity: 100% (all images are 512x512)

Interpretation: Dataset quality is high. Only 0.25% corruption rate,
all corrupted images are automatically skipped during loading.

KEY FINDINGS & IMPLICATIONS¶

In [11]:
print("""
╔══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗
║           KEY FINDINGS & PREPROCESSING IMPLICATIONS          ║
╚══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝

1. DATASET SIZE
   Finding   : 5,698 valid images across 4 classes
   Decision  : 70/15/15 train/val/test split applied
   
2. CLASS IMBALANCE
   Finding   : Notumor (1593) vs Glioma (1321) — ~20% gap
   Decision  : Use class weights in CrossEntropyLoss to 
               penalize majority class predictions

3. IMAGE RESOLUTION
   Finding   : All images are exactly 512x512 pixels
   Decision  : Resize to 224x224 for MLP compatibility
               and memory efficiency

4. BRIGHTNESS VARIATION
   Finding   : Glioma images significantly darker (mean ~28)
               vs Notumor (mean ~57)
   Decision  : Apply ImageNet normalization (mean/std) to
               standardize pixel distributions

5. CORRUPTED IMAGES
   Finding   : 14 corrupted images (0.25% of dataset)
   Decision  : Automatically skip during loading via
               try/except in MRIDataset.__getitem__

6. VISUAL SIMILARITY
   Finding   : Glioma and meningioma look visually similar
   Decision  : MLP baseline expected to struggle — motivates
               CNN with spatial feature extraction in Phase 3

7. DATA AUGMENTATION
   Finding   : MRI scans can appear in different orientations
   Decision  : Apply RandomHorizontalFlip + RandomRotation(15°)
               on training set only

8. NO DUPLICATES
   Finding   : All filenames unique, no duplicate samples found
   Decision  : No deduplication step needed
""")

╔══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗
║           KEY FINDINGS & PREPROCESSING IMPLICATIONS          ║
╚══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝

1. DATASET SIZE
   Finding   : 5,698 valid images across 4 classes
   Decision  : 70/15/15 train/val/test split applied

2. CLASS IMBALANCE
   Finding   : Notumor (1593) vs Glioma (1321) — ~20% gap
   Decision  : Use class weights in CrossEntropyLoss to 
               penalize majority class predictions

3. IMAGE RESOLUTION
   Finding   : All images are exactly 512x512 pixels
   Decision  : Resize to 224x224 for MLP compatibility
               and memory efficiency

4. BRIGHTNESS VARIATION
   Finding   : Glioma images significantly darker (mean ~28)
               vs Notumor (mean ~57)
   Decision  : Apply ImageNet normalization (mean/std) to
               standardize pixel distributions

5. CORRUPTED IMAGES
   Finding   : 14 corrupted images (0.25% of dataset)
   Decision  : Automatically skip during loading via
               try/except in MRIDataset.__getitem__

6. VISUAL SIMILARITY
   Finding   : Glioma and meningioma look visually similar
   Decision  : MLP baseline expected to struggle — motivates
               CNN with spatial feature extraction in Phase 3

7. DATA AUGMENTATION
   Finding   : MRI scans can appear in different orientations
   Decision  : Apply RandomHorizontalFlip + RandomRotation(15°)
               on training set only

8. NO DUPLICATES
   Finding   : All filenames unique, no duplicate samples found
   Decision  : No deduplication step needed
In [ ]:
